creator: rmacklem@FreeBSD.org
text:
This is fixed as a part of the patch I've generated for
conversion of d_fileno to 64bits.
Also, this patch includes padding before d_name, so that this
"assumption" (ie. no padding after d_name) still applies.
It is rumoured that some userland software also assumes no
padding after d_name.

And, yes, the patch does use offsetof(), which was
suggested by Philip Guenther, who did the conversion for OpenBSD.
(I think the patch deletes the DIRHDSIZ macro for all cases of it
 and uses a macro called _GENERIC_DIRLEN() defined in sys/dirent.h.)

Thanks for doing this PR, since it will remind us about this as
the patch for 64bit ino_t moves forward.
